title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A classic American novel set in the Jazz Age, exploring themes of wealth, love, and the American Dream. The story follows Jay Gatsby's pursuit of his lost love, Daisy Buchanan, against the backdrop of 1920s Long Island.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A gripping tale of racial injustice and childhood innocence in the American South. Through the eyes of young Scout Finch, we witness her father's courageous defense of a black man falsely accused of a crime.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian social science fiction novel and cautionary tale about totalitarianism. Set in Airstrip One, formerly Great Britain, the novel follows Winston Smith as he rebels against the oppressive Party.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A romantic novel of manners that follows the character development of Elizabeth Bennet, the protagonist. The novel explores themes of love, reputation, and class in Georgian England.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
description: A story about teenage rebellion and alienation. Holden Caulfield's journey through New York City after being expelled from prep school has resonated with generations of readers.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A fantasy adventure about Bilbo Baggins, a hobbit who embarks on an unexpected journey with a group of dwarves to reclaim their mountain home from the dragon Smaug.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: The first book in the beloved Harry Potter series. Follow young Harry as he discovers he's a wizard and begins his magical education at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry.

title: The Da Vinci Code
author: Dan Brown
description: A mystery thriller that follows symbologist Robert Langdon as he investigates a murder in the Louvre Museum in Paris, uncovering a conspiracy involving the Catholic Church.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: An exploration of the history of the human species, from the evolution of archaic human species to the 21st century. Harari examines how biology and history have defined us.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
description: A memoir about a young woman who grows up in a strict and abusive household in rural Idaho but eventually escapes to learn about the wider world through education.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: A practical guide to building good habits and breaking bad ones. Clear reveals how tiny changes can lead to remarkable results through the compound effect of small improvements.

title: The Lean Startup
author: Eric Ries
description: A revolutionary approach to building and launching successful startups. Ries introduces the concept of validated learning and rapid experimentation to create sustainable businesses.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: A science fiction masterpiece set on the desert planet Arrakis. The story follows Paul Atreides as his family takes control of the planet, the only source of the valuable spice melange.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A philosophical novel about a young Andalusian shepherd who travels from Spain to Egypt in search of treasure. Along the way, he learns important life lessons about following one's dreams.

title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
author: Stieg Larsson
description: A gripping mystery thriller about journalist Mikael Blomkvist and hacker Lisbeth Salander as they investigate a decades-old disappearance in a wealthy Swedish family.

title: Becoming
author: Michelle Obama
description: The memoir of former First Lady Michelle Obama. She describes her roots and how she found her voice, as well as her time in the White House and her public health campaign.

title: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
author: Stephen R. Covey
description: A business and self-help book that presents an approach to being effective in attaining goals by aligning oneself to what Covey calls 'true north' principles.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: A groundbreaking tour of the mind explaining the two systems that drive the way we think. System 1 is fast, intuitive, and emotional; System 2 is slower, more deliberative, and more logical.

title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: A novel about all the choices that go into a life well lived. Between life and death there is a library, and within that library, the shelves go on forever. Every book provides a chance to try another life.

title: Where the Crawdads Sing
author: Delia Owens
description: A coming-of-age story and murder mystery set in the marshes of North Carolina. The story follows Kya, the 'Marsh Girl,' who raises herself in the wilderness after being abandoned by her family.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
description: A psychological thriller about a woman who shoots her husband and then never speaks another word. A criminal psychotherapist becomes obsessed with uncovering her motive.

title: Good to Great
author: Jim Collins
description: A management book examining how companies transition from being good companies to great companies, and how most companies fail to make the transition.

title: The Power of Now
author: Eckhart Tolle
description: A guide to spiritual enlightenment that emphasizes the importance of living in the present moment and transcending thoughts of the past and future.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: A landmark volume in science writing that explores the universe's fundamental questions, from the Big Bang to black holes, in accessible language for general readers.

title: The Hunger Games
author: Suzanne Collins
description: A dystopian novel set in a post-apocalyptic nation where teenagers are forced to compete in a televised battle to the death. Katniss Everdeen volunteers to take her sister's place in the games.
